Ad Text:
We've been proudly bringing joy to tables and smiles to faces with the delicious food we've been crafting for over 100 years. 

A large part of our strong growth and success is because we're always looking for new ways to do things and thinking about how we can create a better, more sustainable future world together.

Our success as an industry leader in providing deliciously good food means we can continue to invest in our future as we work towards becoming Australia and New Zealand's first choice for poultry. 



Your opportunity to join the Growth team in North Ryde

We are looking for a commercially minded senior analyst to join the Growth team in North Ryde, a role offering real exposure, variety, and the opportunity to help shape meaningful business decisions.This is a hands-on role where you’ll partner closely with stakeholders across the business, structuring ambiguous problems, building rigorous analytical models, and turning data into clear strategic and commercial recommendations that will influence the direction of the business. 

You will work with a range of stakeholders to tackle complex and consequential decisions. This isn’t a reporting role; you will use data to solve complex problems, build scenario models, and support commercial and strategic decision-making.  

You’ll bring depth across growth evaluation, financial and scenario modelling, and supply chain strategy — connecting operational realities to enterprise-level choices.

What you’ll do
Build rigorous financial, scenario, sensitivity, and decision-tree models to evaluate strategic options and surface key trade-offs for senior decision-makers
Provide analysis that contributes to pricing, margin improvement, and commercial initiatives, and support commercial agreements  
Partner with teams across strategy, operations, supply chain, and finance to solve business challenges and deliver commercial analysis and insight that supports strategy execution and confident decision-making
Analyse competitive, consumer, regulatory, and macroeconomic trends and translate them into clear strategic implications
Apply strong commercial thinking to connect the numbers to real business outcomes
Synthesise large volumes of internal and external data into concise, insight-led outputs for executive and leadership audiences
What you’ll bring
5–8 years in strategy, management consulting, or a senior analytical role within a complex commercial environment
A track record of structuring and solving complex problems — from defining the question to delivering the recommendation
Strong commercial acumen — an instinct for what drives value and the ability to connect analytical outputs to real business decisions
Advanced financial and analytical modelling capability, with strong data visualisation skills and comfort with large, complex datasets
An ability to leverage data, AI, and emerging tools to sharpen analysis and generate faster, deeper insight
Experience preparing executive-level communication — clear, concise, and persuasive
Proactive mindset, strong communication skills and ability to influence stakeholders
Background in food manufacturing, FMCG, supply chain, or agribusiness is advantageous, not essential
Why this role

You’ll have genuine influence on decisions that matter — working at pace, alongside senior leaders, on problems that don’t have easy answers. If you’re intellectually curious, commercially sharp, and energised by turning complexity into clarity, this is the environment for you.

We’re looking for people who are curious, caring, courageous, and committed, people who want to contribute their best work every day and help deliver food that’s… Always Good

Build data pipelines and deliver high-quality statistical insights.



Your new company
You'll join a large, stable organisation with a strong national footprint, known for investing in high-quality data, rigorous analysis, and evidence‑based decision-making. The culture values technical expertise, collaboration, and delivering trusted insights at scale.


Your new role
This role requires a specialist Data Scientist to support the ongoing development and use of a large-scale national data asset. The position is responsible for designing, building, and maintaining high-quality, robust administrative data pipelines that underpin outcome analysis across complex education and workforce pathways. It also requires advanced econometric expertise, including the application of causal and statistical methods to measure outcomes and support modelling activities.
The role is critical to delivering high-quality analytical outputs and to maintaining and leveraging a core data asset that underpins a wide range of analytical reports, system performance measures, and strategic insights at a national level.
The position applies advanced data engineering, data architecture, and confidentiality management skills to integrate and manage complex administrative datasets, ensuring accurate, reliable, and timely outputs for government and other stakeholders. By sustaining this infrastructure and applying advanced analytical capability, the role enables the consistent delivery of trusted, evidence‑based insights to support policy development and decision-making.



Key responsibilities:

Design, build and maintain high-quality, robust administrative data pipelines to support outcome analysis across complex domains.
Manage the ingestion, transformation and integration of large, complex administrative datasets from multiple sources, ensuring accuracy, consistency and reliability.
Apply advanced econometric, causal inference and statistical methods to measure outcomes and support evaluation and modelling activities.
Implement data quality controls, validation processes and documentation standards to ensure the integrity, confidentiality and reproducibility of analytical outputs.
Support the delivery of critical analytical outputs, including performance measures, reports and insights for government and other stakeholders.
What you'll need to succeed


Essential Criteria:

Demonstrated advanced coding skills across multiple programming languages.
Strong capability in database management, including working with large scale administrative datasets, data extraction, transformation and integration, and ensuring data quality, integrity and performance.
Proven experience designing, building and maintaining high quality, robust administrative data pipelines to support analysis across complex policy or service delivery domains.
Demonstrated experience applying advanced econometric and statistical methods, including causal inference techniques, to support evaluation and impact analysis in policy or service delivery settings.
Desirable criteria

Demonstrated experience working with vocational education and training (VET) and higher education data, supported by strong subject matter knowledge across both sectors and an understanding of relevant policy and administrative contexts.
Demonstrated practical experience working within the ABS Person Level Integrated Data Asset (PLIDA), including extracting detailed analytical outputs while adhering to ABS legislative, privacy and statistical disclosure control requirements.
What you'll get in return


Competitive Hourly rate

Flexible working arrangements

2x12 Month Extension Options available at department's discretion


If you're interested in this role, please send an up-to-date resume to george.pinkerton@hays.com.au

If this job isn't quite right for you, but you are looking for a new position, please contact me for a confidential discussion about your career.

We're seeking a Senior Business Analyst to join a large-scale transformation program focused on complex decommissioning initiatives. This is a business-facing role where you'll work closely with stakeholders to define requirements, map processes, and drive future-state outcomes. You'll act as the bridge between business and technology, ensuring clarity, alignment, and successful delivery across multiple workstreams.

Key Responsibilities:

Lead business analysis activities across complex decommissioning initiatives
Elicit, analyse and document business requirements through workshops, interviews and process walkthroughs
Develop current and future state process maps, identifying gaps, risks and opportunities
Facilitate workshops to align business and technology stakeholders
Assess solution options, impacts and cross-program dependencies
Act as the conduit between business and technology teams to ensure clarity of requirements
Support change management activities, including scoping and training inputs
Maintain high-quality documentation across Confluence, JIRA and Visio
Contribute to a strong risk-aware approach across the program
Participate in program governance and key delivery forums
Provide oversight across BA deliverables to ensure consistency, quality and collaboration

About You:

Proven experience as a Senior Business Analyst in complex environments
Strong skills in requirements elicitation, process mapping and documentation
Experience driving business-led discovery and process transformation
Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills
Ability to work autonomously and manage competing priorities
Strong facilitation skills with the ability to drive alignment and decisions
Experience identifying risks, dependencies and business impacts

Nice to Have:

Experience in large-scale transformation or decommissioning programs
Background in enterprise or complex organisational environments

Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples are encouraged to apply.

To apply please click apply or email Zach Cosgrave on zach.cosgrave@robertwalters.com.au for a confidential discussion.

About the role

The Data Office is the central hub for managing, protecting, and maximising the value of TFA's data, enabling better decision-making, transparency, and customer outcomes. There are currently 5 cross functional teams / squads in Data Office.

The Senior Business Analyst (SBA) will play a key role in supporting data-driven initiatives within the Data Office, working across discovery, delivery, and validation. The role requires strong collaboration with business stakeholders and cross-functional data teams to ensure high-quality, well-defined, and actionable data requirements that enable trusted insights and seamless integration with front-end/domain initiatives.

This is a 12-month contract based in Sydney.

How you'll make an impact

Elicit, analyse, and refine business needs into clear user stories and data requirements
Define and document data requirements, business rules, and data mappings
Interpret and analyse data models, data flows, and datasets to support solution design
Conduct data mapping and support integration activities (e.g., legacy to new datasets, financial data transitions)
Prepare integration specifications and ensure alignment across systems and platforms
Collaborate closely with stakeholders and delivery teams including Scrum Masters, Architects, Data Engineers, BI Analysts, Data Scientists, and QA
Partner with Architects to ensure requirements are clear, unambiguous, and consumable by both technical and non-technical audiences
Support data validation, testing, and quality assurance activities to ensure accuracy and integrity
Contribute end-to-end across Data Office initiatives, including reporting and analytics requirements
Participate actively in PI Planning to align priorities, dependencies, and delivery outcomes
Cross-collaborate with BAs, PMs, and domain teams to ensure seamless integration between front-end initiatives and data solutions
Manage and engage multiple stakeholders, ensuring clear communication, alignment, and expectation management

Focus Areas

Data discovery, analysis, and delivery across initiatives
Data modelling, mapping, and integration
Data validation, quality, and governance considerations
Supporting analytics, reporting, and data platform use cases
Enabling high-quality, trusted data for business insights

Your attributes

Proven experience as a Business Analyst in data-driven environments
Strong ability to translate complex data concepts into clear and actionable business requirements
Hands-on experience with data mapping, integration specifications, and data migration activities
Solid understanding of data structures, data flows, and business rules
Experience working across the data lifecycle, including data quality and governance principles
Strong stakeholder management and communication skills across business and technical teams
Ability to context switch effectively across discovery, delivery, and validation phases
Demonstrated collaboration with cross-functional data teams including engineers, analysts, and scientists

Desirable:

Experience working with Data Scientists and ML Engineers to shape AI/ML requirements
Familiarity with BI/analytics tools and data visualisation best practices
Experience in financial services, accounting, treasury, or risk domains
Exposure to SAP environments
Background in business intelligence and analytics

Help us deliver a better tomorrow
Australia Post is delivering for all Australians. From the vehicles that we drive, to the small businesses that make our communities thrive. We’re delivering for the environment, for our communities, for our customers and for our people. We’re moving forward and we want you to come along for the ride. 

About the opportunity

This is a chance to turn data into decisions that matter, to take insights from dashboards to decisions. As Insight Visualisation Lead, you’ll help leaders see what is happening in the business, why it matters, and what to do next. You’ll build dashboards, reports and insight packs that support better commercial and operational decisions across Freight Services.

You’ll join the high performing, market leading, Strategic Insights & Analytics team and work closely with product, pricing, sales, marketing and strategy teams. If you enjoy solving business problems, working with stakeholders and making complex information easy to understand, this role will give you plenty of scope to add value.

What you’ll deliver with us

You’ll deliver clear, accurate and useful reporting that helps the business make better decisions. Success in this role means your insights lead to action, improve performance, and help stakeholders focus on what matters most. The role reports to the Group Manager, Performance & Insight.

You’ll also

Build dashboards, reports and visualisations that turn data into practical business insights.
Work with stakeholders to understand their questions and turn them into reporting solutions that meet their needs.
Analyse performance, identify trends and root causes, and provide recommendations that support action and improvement.
Use cutting edge AI solutions in order to deliver at scale.

About you

You’re commercially minded, curious and good at making the complex simple. You know how to turn data into a story that people can quickly understand and use. You ask good questions, focus on outcomes, and enjoy working with others to solve problems.

You’re confident working across different teams and priorities. You bring structure, sound judgement and a practical approach. You care about quality, can manage deadlines, and keep looking for better ways to improve reporting and insights.

You’ll also need

Strong experience in data analysis, reporting and business intelligence including data visualisation tools. 
Strong communication skills, with the ability to present insights clearly to non-technical audiences and senior stakeholders.
A strong curiosity and ability to use cutting edge AI to help delve into insights & the practical application of insights to make recommendations. 

How we’ll deliver for you

A role where your work has a clear impact on business decisions, priorities and performance.
Variety and exposure across product, pricing, sales, marketing and strategy teams.
The chance to improve how reporting and insights are delivered, and help lift decision making across the business.
Talk to us about what flexibility means to you and our hybrid work environment. 
Save big savings with the PostPerks platform and app, helping you save every day across retailers online and on the high street. 
A great workplace culture in a company with a defined strategy and plan for the future. 

We’re delivering together

Job Title: Senior Manager - Market & Financial Risk Oversight and Assurance (Line 2)
Ad Text:
My client is looking for an experienced and strategic Senior Manager, Market & Financial Risk Oversight and Assurance to support the Head of Market Risk in leading the function. In this position, you will play a critical part in ensuring a robust and effective risk management framework across the Bank. You will bring deep expertise across liquidity, funding, interest rate risk, counterparty credit risk, capital management, and financial forecasting, providing independent challenge and insightful analysis. As a trusted subject matter expert, you will influence key forums including the Asset and Liability Committee and the Board, while championing a strong, positive risk culture.
  
About the Position
The Market Risk team is responsible for analysing, reviewing and overseeing market and financial risks across the Bank. Your key responsibilities in this position include:
  
Risk Oversight & Assurance

Overseeing Treasury and Capital risk activities including liquidity, funding, capital adequacy, IRRBB, and counterparty credit risk
Providing independent challenge on the effectiveness of risk frameworks
Supporting oversight of risk models and regulatory compliance including APRA standards
Leading reviews of risk self-assessments to ensure strong control environments
  
Risk Analytics, Reporting & Insights

Delivering high-quality risk reporting to regulators, Board, committees, and executive leadership
Analysing trends, concentrations, and emerging risks that impact the Bank’s risk profile
Ensuring risk events are accurately recorded, monitored, escalated, and reported
Leading targeted initiatives to enhance risk reporting and insights
  
Stakeholder Engagement

Partnering with business stakeholders to embed risk considerations into strategic decisions
Influencing outcomes through strong collaboration and clear communication
  
Continuous Improvement

Driving innovation and continuous enhancement of risk practices
Promoting a culture of constructive challenge and accountability
Ensuring that business changes are effectively integrated into risk frameworks
  
About You
To be successful in this position you will bring:

10+ years of experience in market risk, treasury risk, or balance sheet risk within an Australian regulated financial institution
Strong analytical expertise across financial instruments and risk types
Deep knowledge of liquidity, capital, IRRBB and counterparty credit risk
Proven leadership experience in developing risk frameworks aligned to Board risk appetite
Strong understanding of financial services regulation and compliance
Excellent stakeholder management and influencing skills
Strong communication skills with the ability to translate complex risk concepts
Highly analytical and data-driven mindset with continuous improvement focus
Ability to manage multiple priorities, projects, and stakeholders in a fast paced environment
  
If you would like more information or for a confidential discussion, please call James Wilson on 0414516089 or by email james.wilson@troocoo.com

This is a full-time five-year contract with the possibility of extension.

THE WORKING ENVIRONMENT

The Corruption and Crime Commission (CCC) is a leading anti-corruption agency whose primary purpose is to strengthen the integrity of the public sector, reduce corruption and disrupt criminal activity for the benefit of our community.

The Assessment and Oversight directorate is a multi-functional team working collaboratively across the Commission and the Western Australian (WA) public sector through its assessment, oversight and research and engagement functions.  The directorate manages the assessment of serious misconduct allegations, oversees action taken by public sector agencies into alleged serious misconduct and conducts research and analysis to inform Commission priorities and assist the public sector to mitigate serious misconduct.

THE OPPORTUNITY

As Assistant Director – Research and Assessments, you will play a key leadership role in guiding both the Research and Engagement team and the Assessment teams. You will provide strategic direction, ensure the integrity and effectiveness of assessment processes, and lead the development of innovative approaches that expand our prevention and education function and enhance public sector capability to reduce corruption.  

You will work closely with senior stakeholders across the Commission and the broader WA public sector, contributing to informed decision‑making, high‑quality reporting, and the continuous improvement of assessment and research practices. This is a rare chance to influence systemic integrity outcomes across government and support the Commission’s role in reducing misconduct and strengthening public trust.

If you are a collaborative leader with strong judgement, a strategic mindset, and a passion for public sector integrity, this role offers the opportunity to make a meaningful impact across the sector.

The Commission requires applicants to be Australian citizens or be immediately eligible for citizenship, and appointment is conditional on satisfactory completion of a rigorous security clearance process.

The Commission is an equal opportunity employer and encourages people with disability, Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people and persons from culturally diverse backgrounds to apply.

To learn more about the Commission and what we have to offer, please visit our website or you can follow us on Linkedin.

DO YOU HAVE ANY QUESTIONS ABOUT THE POSITION?

Enquiries about the position should be directed to Tracey Polmear, Director Assessments and Oversight on (08) 9215 4807.

HOW TO APPLY

Click on the “Apply for Job” button and attach the following:

A current and comprehensive CV which includes the names and contact details (phone number and email address) of two recent, work-related referees (including your current line manager)
A two-page statement addressing the role specific capability requirements from the Job Description Form (JDF) as listed below:
Significant experience in partnering with senior management to drive strategic priorities.
Experience leading teams to analyse complex problems and presentation of appropriate recommendations, based on consistent decision making.
Significant experience in managing prioritisation and continual development, specifically in the areas of research practices and reporting.

Applications must be submitted online – hard copy, late or proforma applications will not be accepted.   

If you are having any problems with the system, you may call Human Resources on 9215 4888.

Closing date:  Monday 27 April 2026 at 4pm

Responsibilities
Unique opportunity with Aon’s Reinsurance team to support the comprehensive approach to risk assessment, risk transfer and risk mitigation
Effective catastrophe management is an essential component of an insurer’s risk management program.
Open to applicants Australia wide, Aon offers flexible working

Aon is in the business of better decisions

At Aon, we shape decisions for the better to protect and enrich the lives of people around the world.

As an organization, we are united through trust as one inclusive team and we are passionate about helping our colleagues and clients succeed.

 

What the day will look like

You will translate complex model outputs into clear, actionable insights to support client results, strategy, and capital decisions.  Using deep expertise across natural perils models and actuarial techniques, our analysts don’t just run numbers – they push the boundaries of what the models can do. By creatively overcoming data gaps and model limitations, they deliver a powerful, end‑to‑end view of each client’s portfolio and its true risk profile.

Constantly connected to all leading modelling firms, the team is at the forefront of industry change as it embraces emerging technologies, advanced analytics, and automation to continuously enhance the speed, accuracy, and impact of their insights for clients.

Key responsibilities include -  

Translating complex technical findings into clear insights and recommendations for internal and external stakeholders
Developing effective working relationships with internal and external clients; including managing client projects with overseas catastrophe modelling teams
Working with modelled outputs to determine suitable reinsurance strategies to mitigate all sources of loss (catastrophe, risk and attritional).
Refining catastrophe models and outputs by analysing clients’ historical data and latest scientific research.
Actively contributing to advanced analytics, climate and natural perils advisory projects.

How this opportunity is different

Work at the intersection of the science, risk and business with a direct impact on the management of natural hazards for our clients in Australia and New Zealand.

Together with our actuaries and brokers, our catastrophe team helps clients navigate the challenges of natural peril risk. This includes support for client reinsurance placements and optimising risk transfer strategies, plus on-going research into climate trends and catastrophe model adjustments.

 

 Skills and experience that will lead to success

Comfortable in client-facing, collaborative, consulting environment with excellent communication skills (both oral and written)
The ability to work well within a team, a curious mindset and strong problem-solving and analytical skills
Experience interpreting catastrophe modelling outputs and deliverables (preferred)
Insurance or reinsurance experience
Excellent organisation skills and ability to work under pressure
Self-discipline to manage own workflow and deadlines 
Advanced Excel skills and strong a working knowledge of MS Word, SQL and PowerPoint
Experience with Python, R, Tableau or AI tools such as databricks, an advantage but not essential
Tertiary qualifications in relevant field of study – natural sciences, engineering, actuarial science, mathematics or statistics

How we support our colleagues

In addition to our comprehensive benefits package, we encourage an inclusive workforce. Plus, our agile environment allows you to manage your wellbeing and work/life balance, ensuring you can be your best self at Aon. Furthermore, all colleagues enjoy two “Global Wellbeing Days” each year, encouraging you to take time to focus on yourself.  We offer a variety of working style solutions for our colleagues as well. 

 

Our continuous learning culture inspires and equips you to learn, share and grow, helping you achieve your fullest potential. As a result, at Aon, you are more connected, more relevant, and more valued.

 

Aon values an innovative and inclusive workplace where all colleagues feel empowered to be their authentic selves. Aon is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace.

 

Aon provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, colour, religion, creed, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, veteran, marital, domestic partner status, or other legally protected status. 

 

We are committed to providing equal employment opportunities and fostering an inclusive workplace. If you require accommodations during the application or interview process, please let us know. You can request accommodations by emailing us at ReasonableAccommodations@Aon.com or your recruiter. We will work with you to meet your needs and ensure a fair and equitable experience.

Take hold of a monster opportunity and join our Investment Operations and Performance team as a Senior Specialist - Valuations. If you’re a good fit for this role and live in Brisbane or Sydney, we’d love you to apply. 

Why join us?

You'll get 14% super as standard, to help your future come alive.
Unleash your potential and build a career to be proud of through excellent training and development opportunities.
Balance your work between in-person days at our purpose-built hubs and working from home.
In the office, enjoy end of trip facilities, a yoga and multi-faith room, parent and carer rooms, quiet rooms, BrewHub coffee facilities, and collaborative team spaces.

About the role

We’re all about helping our members make the most of their money. And while they go after their goals, you can too. As a Senior Specialist - Valuations, you’ll play an important role in managing valuation risk across the fund and ensuring the accurate and timely reporting of unlisted asset valuations to committees that oversee valuation governance. You will partner closely with investment teams, external managers and independent valuers to ensure strong, consistent valuation processes and high‑quality governance outcomes.




Day to day, you'll:

Oversee the valuation process across all investment asset classes, ensuring compliance with best practice and regulatory standards.
Review and challenge valuations from independent valuers and external managers, ensuring accuracy, strong judgement, and clear supporting documentation.
Contribute to the management of the composition of the Fund’s panel of valuers including coordinating RFPs, overseeing the RFP process of external managers for new assets or when rotation is required, all the way through to the engagement of an independent valuer.
Monitor markets and industries to identify when out‑of‑cycle valuations may be required.
Assess the impact of macro‑economic factors (e.g. interest rates, inflation, growth, and geopolitical risks) on asset valuations.
Support the Valuation Committee and contribute to Board and Committee papers.
Conduct valuation governance reviews for new and existing external managers to drive continuous improvement.

This role is available as a permanent opportunity.

About you

It goes without saying you'll be a great communicator with top notch interpersonal skills. We'll also expect you to pick up problems and come up with quick, creative ways to solve them. It's quite likely you tick some of the following boxes too:  

Bachelor’s degree in finance, economics, accounting, or a related field.
Strong analytical and quantitative skills.
Proficiency in excel based analysis, financial modelling and valuation techniques.
Experience in managing multiple projects with a number of stakeholders and service providers.
Ability to work collaboratively with cross-functional teams.
Knowledge of relevant accounting standards.

About us

Our 2.4 million members trust us to take care of over $330 billion in retirement savings. And we help them take charge of their finances and face the future with confidence. Our inclusive culture means you’ll be valued and heard in a respectful workplace. We've created an environment where you can be yourself and do amazing work. It's that simple.

Apply online now

Everyone is welcome to apply. We value diverse thinking, cultures, perspectives, backgrounds, and abilities. Awaken your career at Australian Retirement Trust and apply now. Applications close Monday, 27 April 2026.

We don’t want to leave you with a monstrous wait. So, you’ll hear from us after the closing date to find out whether you’ve made it through to the next round or not. Being fair and up-front is important to us – and recruitment is no different.

A global, digitally enabled business that empowers a brighter future by connecting millions of people with their assets – safely, securely and responsibly. Through our two businesses MUFG Retirement Solutions and MUFG Corporate Markets, we partner with a diversified portfolio of global clients to provide robust, efficient and scalable services, purpose-built solutions and modern technology platforms that deliver world class outcomes and experiences. A member of MUFG, a global financial group, we help manage regulatory complexity, improve data management and connect people with their assets, through exceptional user experience that leverages the expertise of our people combined with scalable technology, digital connectivity and data insights.

About MUFG Pension & Market Services (Optional)


MUFG Pension & Market Services is a global business connecting millions of people with their assets – safely, securely, and responsibly. We have over 6500 employees across 4 continents and continue to grow every day.

MUFG Retirement Solutions is the largest business under MUFG Pension and Market Services and we are the leading administrator for super funds in Australia. Many of our clients are household names. We are well positioned within a growing superannuation industry, combining our rich legacy with a fast-paced environment that enables rapid career growth.

We are a diverse team with employees from various cultural and linguistic backgrounds, and we make sure to celebrate the successes of our clients and our people.

 

The Role


We’re looking for a highly skilled Senior Business Analyst to join our Portfolio Management team within MUFG Retirement Solutions. In this role, you will work closely with Project Managers, business leaders, cross-functional teams, and senior stakeholders to ensure business and technical requirements are captured, documented, and delivered across the full project lifecycle.

You will play a key part in shaping analysis practices, supporting continuous improvement across the Portfolio and Squad teams, and contributing to a culture of collaboration, quality, and delivery excellence. Your ability to navigate ambiguity, lead workshops, influence stakeholders, and produce high-quality documentation will be essential to your success.


Key Accountabilities and main responsibilities

 

Lead the definition of business problems by conducting in‑depth analysis across technical and non‑technical inputs. 
Develop clear, detailed business and technical requirements ensuring solutions meet objectives and quality standards. 
Maintain full traceability from requirements through development and testing. 
Facilitate workshops and discussions to gather, validate, and prioritise requirements. 
Analyse data to validate opportunities, inform decision‑making, and support business cases. 
Model business processes and identify improvements to enhance efficiency and customer experience. 
Assess impacted systems to determine required changes and guide developers on key considerations for accurate build. 
Produce high‑quality documentation including Business Requirements, Functional Specifications, and screen flows. 
Manage and maintain requirement artefacts to build organisational knowledge and support future project work. 
Analyse backlogs to identify synergies and improve efficiency across projects and workstreams. 
Contribute to and facilitate BA Centre of Excellence discussions and uplift initiatives. 
Support and/or lead functional testing activities, ensuring evidence, documentation, and strong traceability are maintained. 
Develop business‑level user acceptance criteria and support business readiness activities to ensure smooth BAU transition. 
Collaborate closely with technical and business stakeholders to review priorities and ensure alignment. 
Provide coaching, guidance, and leadership to team members, modelling strong communication and collaboration. 
Identify, assess, and manage project risks and issues, and champion strong RAID management practices. 
Provide recommendations for portfolio‑level improvements, efficiency gains, and enhanced delivery outcomes.




 

Experience & Personal Attributes

 

5+ years Technical Business Analyst experience, covering both business and technical requirements. 
Strong grounding in project management fundamentals (estimation, planning, resource management, risk & issue management, communications). 
Experience with project management and scheduling tools. 
Proficiency creating reports, charts, and presentations for executive stakeholders. 
Experienced user of Microsoft Office, ServiceNow, Jira, and SharePoint.
Strong understanding and experience with Agile methodologies. 
Ability to work confidently across multiple business and technical teams. 
Strong communication skills, with experience presenting to both large and small groups. 
Excellent analytical, problem‑solving, and documentation skills. 
Ability to manage competing priorities and adapt to change. 
Testing experience will be highly advantageous. 
Highly self‑driven, collaborative, and able to navigate

.




 

Some of our Employment Benefits

FlexiWorks – enabling our employees to work in the office and at home
Purchased Leave, Parental Leave, Volunteer Leave, Gender transition leave, Well-being leave
Employee Recognition Program - to recognise people who are demonstrating our values and Company purpose.

We are an inclusive employer whose people work collaboratively. We encourage, support and value the various talents and perspectives of our people and promote a flexible and blended work environment where our people can thrive, and their wellbeing is supported. We know that diversity drives better client outcomes, continuous improvement, and growth. Be part of our company and together we will achieve our full potential.

We treat all individuals fairly and equitably and do not discriminate on the basis of diverse characteristics including, but not limited to gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, ethnicity, cultural background, physical abilities/disabilities, religious or political belief, marital or family status or carers responsibilities.


How to apply


Ready to take the next step in your career? Apply now with your resume and detailed cover letter highlighting your skills, experience, and why you would be a good fit for the role.

Candidates must have the relevant work rights to be considered for an opportunity at MUFG Pension & Market Services. Successful applicants will be required to complete background screening prior to commencement of employment.

Ad Text:
As one of Australia’s largest super funds, we’re focused on delivering exceptional, personalised experiences that genuinely improve financial outcomes for our members. We’re now seeking an experienced Manager – Marketing Technology and Journeys to lead our MarTech and AdTech ecosystem and play a critical role in shaping the future of member engagement.

Why join us?

You'll get 14% super as standard, to help your future come alive.
Unleash your potential and build a career to be proud of through excellent training and development opportunities.
Balance your work between in-person days at our purpose-built hubs and working from home.
In the office, enjoy end of trip facilities, a yoga and multi-faith room, parent and carer rooms, quiet rooms, BrewHub coffee facilities, and collaborative team spaces.

About the role

We’re all about helping our members make the most of their money. And while they go after their goals, you can too. Reporting into the Senior Manager - Marketing Technology and Analytics, you'll be responsible for overseeing ART’s end‑to‑end marketing and advertising technology stack, ensuring it is high‑performing, well‑integrated and aligned to business strategy.


You’ll define and deliver future‑state MarTech roadmaps, manage platforms such as the Adobe Experience Platform and the Google Marketing Platform, and lead the data and audience team to enable sophisticated audience management, cross‑channel journeys and privacy‑safe measurement.


This is a highly collaborative role, partnering closely with IT, Digital, Design and Innovation teams to deliver seamless member experiences while fostering a culture of innovation and agility across marketing.

 

Day to day, you'll:

Define and execute ART’s MarTech and AdTech roadmaps, aligned to marketing and business goals.
Oversee the assessment, implementation and ongoing management of MarTech and AdTech tools including CDPs, journey tools, analytics platforms, clean rooms and ad platforms.
Lead and develop the data and audience team, building robust audience strategies to drive targeting, personalisation and journey effectiveness.
Manage the Google Marketing Platform (GMP) stack and publisher integrations to enable privacy‑safe tracking and measurement.
Ensure strong data governance, data quality and privacy compliance, partnering with IT on data incident management.
Identify and assess emerging technologies and AI, championing innovation within the MarTech ecosystem.
Act as a change agent, embedding agile ways of working and continuous improvement.
Fulfil risk management and Members Best Financial Interests (MBFI) responsibilities in line with enterprise guidelines.

This role is a permanent opportunity. 

📍 Location: Brisbane, Sydney or Melbourne. 

About you

It goes without saying you'll be a great communicator with top notch interpersonal skills. We'll also expect you to pick up problems and come up with quick, creative ways to solve them. It's quite likely you tick some of the following boxes too:  

You bring extensive experience working in marketing technology and content‑led environments.
You’re confident managing complex MarTech and AdTech ecosystems at scale.
You’ve successfully developed and delivered MarTech roadmaps that align to broader business and marketing strategies.
You’ll have hands‑on experience with CDPs, journey orchestration tools, analytics platforms, clean rooms and ad platforms.
You bring strong expertise across the Adobe Experience Platform, Google Marketing Platform, including GA4, DV360, Campaign Manager 360 and SA360.
You have a deep understanding of audience management, data enablement and personalisation, and how these drive effective member journeys.
You’re experienced partnering with IT and digital teams to deliver seamless, integrated technology solutions.
You understand the importance of data governance, privacy and compliance, and you actively manage data quality and risk.

About us

Our 2.4 million members trust us to take care of over $330 billion in retirement savings. And we help them take charge of their finances and face the future with confidence. Our inclusive culture means you’ll be valued and heard in a respectful workplace. We've created an environment where you can be yourself and do amazing work. It's that simple.

Apply online now

Everyone is welcome to apply. We value diverse thinking, cultures, perspectives, backgrounds, and abilities. Awaken your career at Australian Retirement Trust and apply now. Applications close COB Wednesday, 22 April 2026. 

We don’t want to leave you with a monstrous wait. So, you’ll hear from us after the closing date to find out whether you’ve made it through to the next round or not. Being fair and up-front is important to us – and recruitment is no different.

About The Role

Reporting into the Head of Technology within the Generosity Value Stream, you’ll be responsible for leading the delivery execution of model experiments and Gen Platform outcomes aligned to Model Driven Generosity (MDG). The MDG initiatives focus on enhancing customer experiences through personalised offers and innovative strategies.

You’ll lead a cross‑functional delivery team, enable strong agile ways of working, and partner closely with product, data, and technology stakeholders to deliver value at pace while balancing platform health and long‑term sustainability.

This role is a 12-month Fixed-term opportunity.

Responsibilities Include:

Lead and own delivery outcomes for a cross‑functional team, ensuring alignment to MDG objectives and project goals.
Enable and evolve agile ways of working, facilitating ceremonies and empowering teams to self‑manage.
Manage backlogs, delivery plans, risks, dependencies, and release coordination.
Provide clear delivery insights and progress updates to stakeholders across Technology, Product, and Data.
Remove impediments and support teams to make effective delivery commitments.
Partner with other Engineering Managers and leaders to drive cross‑team collaboration and continuous improvement.

About You

While we are big on development and offer on the job training, we want to set you up for success.

You’re a delivery‑focused engineering leader who thrives in complex, fast‑moving environments. You’re comfortable working with ambiguity, confident leading cross‑functional teams, and passionate about agile, value‑driven delivery. You build strong relationships and bring a strong sense of ownership and accountability to everything you do.

What You Will Bring

Proven experience leading delivery or engineering teams in agile, high‑volume environments.
Strong delivery leadership and management skills, with the ability to manage multiple workstreams and stakeholder expectations, driving people to the right outcomes.
An agile mindset, with hands‑on experience facilitating ceremonies and championing agile practices.
Ability to communicate complex technical and delivery concepts clearly to diverse stakeholders.
Understanding of engineering excellence principles and balancing delivery with platform sustainability.

The Perks

We work hard and play hard, so along with a competitive salary and generous performance-based bonus for permanent staff members, here’s some of our other perks:

25 days annual leave (that’s right, an additional week’s leave!)
Hybrid working policy (Monday and Friday from home) and a $1,050 home office allowance for new starters.
Industry leading Parental Leave support program with 26 weeks paid leave for Primary Carers and no wait time. We also have a progressive return to work benefit for your first 6 months back: come back for 3 days, get paid for 4; come back for 4 days, get paid for 5.
Hack Days - where your creativity and collaborative ideas lead to exciting new ideas and projects for our customers.
Access to outstanding career development programs, including expert-led internal training and external learning opportunities (staff allowance for personal development)
Weekly concierge service including free massages and nail appointments – all onsite in our Melbourne office!
Free breakfast, daily fresh fruit and weekly rotating socials, from Thursday office drinks to fun team events
End of trip facilities with onsite table tennis and pool tables
Discounted health insurance and free flu jabs yearly.

We review applications within the first two weeks, so apply now! Check out our Instagram and TikTok (@lifeatsportsbet), LinkedIn, and careers website for more information about our inclusive culture. 

Our Focus on Diversity, Equity & Inclusion

We don’t just support diversity – we celebrate it! This is a workplace where you can thrive and feel safe to bring your whole authentic self to work.  

What’s the Green Tick Feeling?  

Well, it’s different for everyone, yet unites us all. It’s the feeling you get when you succeed, no matter what success looks like to you. It's all the little and big things that make you feel like you’re thriving when working at Sportsbet.   
   
We’re an inclusive employer who welcomes you for who you are, as you are – so, if you require adjustments to the recruitment process, please let us know in your application. We also know flex means different things to different people, so let us know how we can support you to be your best.   

If you think you’d be a great fit but don’t necessarily meet everything in the job description, please still get in touch.

We support growing families with inclusive, carer-neutral paid Parental Leave of up to 16 weeks.

Direct your own development and build future-ready skills with Optus U's industry-focused micro-credentials, developed with La Trobe and Macquarie Universities.

Build meaningful connections through colleague-led networks and diversity initiatives including Culture Connect, Elevate Women, Disability Network, and Express Yourself (LGBTQIA+).

About the Agency
As the steward for digitally enabling Australia’s health system, the Agency plays a key role in coordinating national engagement, delivery, and adoption of digital health to facilitate person-centred, connected healthcare. Our responsibilities span national digital health services and systems, including My Health Record, Electronic Prescriptions, and the 1800Medicare App.

About the Role 
The Research, Analytics and Data (RAD) Branch is seeking a Director, Data Strategy and Governance (EL2) for a full-time ongoing position. Reporting to the Chief Data and Analytics Officer (CDAO), this role supports the CDAO and leads a geographically dispersed team responsible for developing and managing the Agency’s data policy, data strategy and data governance framework, aligned to the Agency’s work program. The role also oversees the delivery of the Agency’s scope of the My Health Record Research and Public Health Program.

Working closely with business areas across the Agency, the Director provides leadership, subject matter expertise and strategic support in relation to data strategy and governance. The role is responsible for defining and implementing data maturity uplift initiatives, including agency-wide data literacy programs, to address strategic data-related risks across the data asset lifecycle. The position supports branches across the Agency to ensure data is trusted, secure and safely accessible, enabling high-quality, informed decision-making. 


Skills and Experience

Demonstrated leadership in reform and change programs, including the design, implementation and embedding of policy, strategy and governance frameworks within multi stakeholder, government environments.
Extensive experience developing and delivering enterprise-wide data strategies, including leading data maturity and data literacy uplift initiatives and driving organisational change.
Demonstrated experience leading teams in a government setting, including geographically dispersed teams, with a focus on capability building and performance.
Strong knowledge of best practice in data strategy, policy and governance, including managing data-related risk to ensure data is trusted, secure and appropriately accessible.
Sound experience in program and project delivery, with a strong understanding of contemporary project management frameworks and methodologies.
Highly developed verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to provide advice, solve complex problems and effectively engage senior leaders and stakeholders.

For all queries relating to the responsibilities of the position, please contact Mike Lau at Mike.Lau@digitalhealth.gov.au.

Our Benefits
We offer a competitive 15.4% superannuation contribution, salary packaging options, and a Christmas closedown with no leave deduction. Our wellbeing program and EAP support a healthy work–life balance, while flexible work arrangements opportunities, including work from home, give you choice in how, when and where you work. You will enjoy modern collaborative workspaces and generous study support to employee who are eligible, as per our study assistance guidelines in our Enterprise Agreement. We also provide a wide range of leave options including parental, cultural, compassionate, emergency response and defence leave. EL staff may access time off in lieu (TOIL) for reasonable additional hours worked, subject to the recording of those hours.

Eligibility
Australian citizenship is mandatory. Candidates must be able to obtain and maintain a national police clearance and the required Negative Vetting 1 security clearance, with the ability to attain a higher-level clearance if needed.

Notes: Order of Merit: Applicants rated as suitable will be placed in a merit pool that may be used to fill similar ongoing or non-ongoing positions throughout the Agency for up to 18 months.

Application
To apply for the role, please submit your resume and a cover letter (1 page maximum) addressing the skills and experience requirements above and outlined in the position description. Please contact the recruitment team at careers@digitalhealth.gov.au with queries relating to the application process.

About the role

Brisbane Location

Hybrid work environment

Microsoft Fabric experience required

We are on the lookout for a dynamic Lead Consultant who is a recognized expert in their field across the broader industry. You will play a key role in shaping and delivering Modern Data Platforms that power Advanced Analytics and AI outcomes. You will lead the end-to-end journey—architecting, designing, developing, testing and delivering innovative, scalable data solutions—while working on complex, high-impact initiatives for leading organizations.

Responsibilities and Accountabilities:

Overseeing the end-to-end delivery, design, and development of Microsoft Fabric-based modern data platform architecture and solutions.

Complete responsibility for technical input, designing and developing smaller solutions and undertaking complex or more significant development activities within a larger project.

Providing mentorship and guidance to more junior AI & Data team members

Assist with building up the Fujitsu AI & Data practice, capabilities and offerings

Assist with pre-sales scoping, shaping of opportunities as well as developing proposals and presenting to clients.

Alert to opportunities for follow-on business and communicates identified new business opportunities for Fujitsu.

Study and remain up to date with relevant technological change and obtain certifications in skills used within the Fujitsu AI & Data team as agreed with your mentor.

Requirements:

Essential

Deep expertise in one or more of:

IoT

Data ingestion

Stream analytics

Microsoft Fabric/Data warehousing

Python

PowerBI / Power Apps

Agile/ Scrum

Good understanding of Data warehousing/Modern Data Platforms

Agile delivery methodology experience

Consulting skills

ETL development expertise

Outstanding technical skills ensuring you deliver value out of the underlying technology

Data Modelling expertise

Ability to manage and lead internal and/or external teams

Manage iterations, scope, priorities, stakeholders, risks, issues and timelines

Forming trusted advisor relationships with key stakeholders and customers

Why Fujitsu?

We are an organization with a strong set of values and a history of respecting fairness and equality, whilst promoting diversity, equity and inclusion. We call this Be Completely You.

Our customers trust us. We have an excellent reputation across the region and globally.

Best in class reward and recognition programs flexible work, volunteering leave and more.

Commitment to Diversity, Equity and Inclusion

As an inclusive employer, Fujitsu aims to recruit a diverse range of talents to help us achieve our purpose. In line with our diversity, equity, and inclusion strategy, we highly welcome applications from women and gender-diverse people; Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people; Māori and Pacific people; LGBTI+ people; people with a disability; culturally and linguistically diverse people; veterans, Australian Defence Force (ADF) and emergency responders. Transgender and gender-diverse applicants can request a copy of our Frequently Asked Questions﻿ to assist with the recruitment journey.

Senior Business Analyst – Insurance Platform Delivery
Location: Sydney CBD (Hybrid, 2–3 days in office)
Salary: Up to $150k base + super

Work on complex insurance platform implementations in a hands-on, client-facing environment.

About the Role
We’re hiring a Senior Business Analyst to support the delivery of insurance platform solutions across multiple clients. You will lead requirements gathering, run workshops, and work closely with product and engineering teams to deliver outcomes across policy, claims, and underwriting.

This is a hands-on role with real ownership, suited to someone comfortable working across the full delivery lifecycle in a consulting-style environment.

Key Responsibilities

Lead requirements gathering across policy administration, claims, and underwriting
Facilitate stakeholder workshops and drive clear outcomes
Translate business needs into functional specifications and user stories
Support solution design, testing, and UAT
Work closely with delivery, product, and engineering teams
Contribute to delivery standards and continuous improvement
Key Requirements

6+ years’ experience as a Business Analyst
Experience in general or commercial insurance
Strong exposure to policy, claims, or underwriting
Experience working in SaaS or platform delivery environments
Client-facing or consulting experience
Strong stakeholder management and communication skills
Experience with Agile delivery, user stories, and backlog management
Tools such as Jira, Confluence, and process mapping tools
Nice to Have

Experience with insurance platforms or policy administration systems
Exposure to integrations, reporting, or automation
Relevant BA or Agile certifications
Why Apply

Work on real platform implementations with direct client exposure
Strong mix of delivery ownership and stakeholder engagement
Modern, growing environment with a clear focus on quality delivery
Hybrid working model in Sydney CBD

THE COMPANY:

This opportunity sits within a large, privately owned Australian organisation with a strong market position, an international footprint, and a genuinely complex operating environment. With multiple business units, established core platforms, and an increasing focus on technology, data and digital capability, this is the kind of business where strong leadership can make a visible difference.

They are well regarded for the quality of their operation, the strength of their brand, and the scale of what they do. Importantly, they are at a point where the next phase of maturity across architecture, engineering and data needs the right leader in place — someone capable of setting direction, creating clarity and building capability for the long term. 


THE OPPORTUNITY:

Reporting to the CIO, this is a high-impact leadership role with enterprise-wide ownership across architecture, data and engineering.

This is not a narrow architecture position. Nor is it a purely strategic role removed from delivery. It is a broad, commercially acute mandate requiring someone who can define the target state, shape the roadmap, establish the standards and governance to support quality delivery, and build the internal capability needed to execute.

You will be central to how the business thinks about technology investment, platform evolution, data maturity and long-term sustainability. You will influence decisions across ERP, digital, e-commerce, integration and data platforms, while partnering with senior stakeholders across operations, finance, digital and risk.

For the right person, this is the type of role that offers real substance — the chance to step into a visible leadership seat, work closely with executive stakeholders, and leave a genuine mark on the future direction of the business. 


DAY TO DAY:

Lead the enterprise-wide architecture, data and engineering agenda

Define and maintain architecture principles, standards and guardrails across applications, integration, data, infrastructure and security

Own the target-state enterprise architecture and multi-year technology roadmap

Advise executive leadership on technology direction, priorities, investment and risk

Govern solution architecture across key initiatives, ensuring alignment with enterprise standards and long-term business objectives

Define and drive the organisation’s data strategy across platforms, analytics, reporting and integration

Establish a clear operating model for data ownership, stewardship, governance and accountability

Build and lead architecture, data engineering, analytics and BI capability across internal teams and partners

Translate operational and commercial priorities into scalable technology and data outcomes

Uplift engineering standards, delivery practices and quality controls across the software lifecycle

Oversee the ongoing evolution of ERP, digital, e-commerce, integration and data platforms

Manage budgets, strategic vendors and delivery partners

Recruit, mentor and lead senior architects, engineers and data specialists 


YOUR BACKGROUND:

This role is likely to suit an established Head of, GM or senior technology leader within the SME space (<500 employees) who has operated across enterprise architecture, data and engineering in a multi-system, business-critical environment.


You will bring:

Demonstrated leadership experience across architecture, data and software engineering

Experience working in complex medium to large organisations with multiple platforms, competing priorities and broad stakeholder groups

The ability to define strategy and translate it into clear, executable roadmaps

Deep understanding of data as an enterprise asset, including governance, quality, security and regulatory considerations

Strong experience building and leading high-performing multi-disciplinary teams

Commercial and financial capability across budget ownership, vendor management and investment prioritisation

The credibility and communication skills to influence senior stakeholders and articulate complex concepts in business terms

Experience across enterprise platforms such as ERP, digital, integration and data

Relevant tertiary qualifications, or equivalent practical leadership experience 


WHAT’S IN IT FOR YOU?

A genuine enterprise leadership role with broad remit and executive visibility

The opportunity to shape architecture, data and engineering strategy in a meaningful way

A business with real scale, complexity and investment behind it

The chance to build capability, improve standards and influence long-term platform direction

Direct exposure to senior decision-makers and business-critical initiatives

A role with the breadth, profile and impact that rarely comes to market


HOW TO APPLY:


For a confidential discussion, please reach out directly or apply via SEEK.

This is an excellent opportunity for a senior technology leader looking for a role with real scope, strong visibility and the ability to influence both strategy and execution.

Super Retail Group is the proud owner of four iconic brands: Supercheap Auto, rebel, BCF and Macpac, and is one of Australia and New Zealand's largest retailers. The talent of our teams and their passion for providing an experience which inspires and enhances our customers’ leisure time drives our culture of innovation and opportunity. 

About the Role

At the centre of a high‑performing Supply Chain is the ability to turn data into action. As Performance & Insights Manager, you’ll shape how insight, analytics, and performance intelligence enable smarter, faster decision‑making across our supply chain operations.

This 14‑month max‑term contract, created as parental leave cover, offers a unique opportunity to make a significant impact in a strategic leadership role. You’ll define and execute the data and performance agenda—bringing together advanced analytics, business intelligence, and predictive modelling to unlock meaningful insights. By enabling leaders with clear, actionable intelligence, you’ll play a critical role in improving operational efficiency, strengthening financial outcomes, and supporting sustainable business growth through data‑driven innovation.

Your key responsibilities will include but not be limited to:

Developing and executing a comprehensive data and performance strategy that aligns with business objectives.

Developing and deploying data models, business intelligence tools, machine learning models and AI-driven solutions to optimize supply chain performance and provide meaningful insights.

Overseeing the enhancement of executive dashboards and real-time reporting tools in Power BI.

Collaborating with cross-functional teams to align data strategies with business priorities.

Leading and mentoring a team of analysts and data professionals, fostering a high-performance culture, as well driving capability development within the analytics function to keep pace with industry trends.

This role offers the flexibility to be based in Brisbane (Strathpine, preferred location), Sydney (Rhodes), or Melbourne with a hybrid work model.

About you

You’re a confident connector who thrives at the intersection of supply chain, commercial, and finance teams, enabling integrated and informed decision‑making. Comfortable engaging with a wide range of stakeholder groups, you communicate effectively across all channels—whether face‑to‑face or digitally. You bring a strong balance of technical capability and people leadership, allowing you to translate complex insights into action while guiding and supporting teams to deliver meaningful outcomes.

In addition, you have proven experience in the following:

A data science or pure maths commercial role, applying data science methods to business problems

Working with complex data sets and data relationships from multiple sources

Creating simple visualisations from complex data sets, matching visualisation style to data types and dimensions

Leading technical teams, ideally data engineers or scientists, in delivering a business service

Working with modern ETL and visualisation tools, preferably with experience in Alteryx and PowerBI, as well as SQL

A qualification in Data Science, Engineering or Mathematics

Highly desirable: Industry experience in Supply Chain, Warehousing or Logistics

WHY JOIN US? 

You’ll get to join for an organisation with a culture that is driven by innovation and creating awesome experiences for our customers and teams, and will get to work alongside a diverse team across Super Retail Group that is representative of the communities we work in. 

Super Retail Group is a Workplace Gender Equality Agency (WGEA) Employer of Choice for Gender Equality citation holder. 

We are committed to your professional development and will support on-going opportunities for both learning and progression. 

You will get access to generous discounts across all Super Retail Group brands, as well as access to our Perks Programme, including discounts on Health and Wellbeing, Entertainment, Travel and Accommodation and more. 

Our Culture of Inclusion

Super Retail Group is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We support, promote and celebrate diversity and we recognise the benefits a culture of inclusion brings to our workplace and customer experience

About the role – Full-time, Permanent

As a Program Test Manager- Payments, Cards and Fraud, join a high-impact transformation role where you will shape enterprise-wide test strategy and elevate QA maturity within a technology-enabled bank. Lead end-to-end testing across complex programs, driving automation, governance, and continuous improvement at scale. Partner with senior stakeholders and build a high-performing QA team to deliver quality outcomes that enhance customer experience.

Key responsibilities include:  

·        Leading enterprise-wide testing for a major transformation program: own the end-to-end test strategy and execution across multiple projects, shaping how quality is delivered in a technology-enabled bank.

·        Defining and embedding QA governance at scale by establishing testing standards, quality gates, and metrics that drive consistency, compliance, and measurable improvements in delivery quality.

·        Driving modernisation of testing practices – champion test automation, CI/CD integration, and continuous improvement initiatives to uplift QA maturity and accelerate delivery cycles.

·        Influencing program success through strategic leadership – partner with senior stakeholders to provide transparent reporting, risk management, and quality insights that directly impact go-live confidence and business outcomes.

·        Building and leading a high-performing QA team - mentoring Test Leads and Analysts, foster a collaborative culture, and create a motivated team aligned to delivering high-quality outcomes.

·        Orchestrate complex delivery across teams and vendors – coordinating cross-functional streams and external partners, ensuring seamless test execution, strong governance, and alignment to program objectives.

About you 

You will succeed in this role if you have:

·        Strong experience in payments, cards, and fraud testing including end-to-end transaction flows, system integration, and user acceptance testing, with a focus on risk, compliance, and production defect prevention. You will bring 4–5 years as a Test Manager leading senior test analysts, with proven success across large, complex programs.

·        Strategic and hands-on in equal measure: able to define test strategy and governance frameworks, while also understanding the detail of execution, tools, and methodologies.

·        Experienced in test automation frameworks and CI/CD integration, with a passion for driving efficiency, coverage, and continuous improvement.

·        People-first leader- a proven ability to build, mentor, and inspire high-performing QA teams, fostering collaboration, accountability, and continuous development.

·        Proactive in identifying risks, managing defects, and enforcing quality gates, ensuring strong governance and minimal production leakage.

·        Confident partnering with senior leadership, business owners, and vendors, translating technical insights into clear, business-focused outcomes.

·        Comfortable using metrics, reporting, and insights to drive decisions, improve testing maturity, and build stakeholder confidence.

Location

Whilst our primary office locations are in Adelaide and Brisbane, we are pleased to be able to consider applications from employees within Australia for this role. We also encourage you to put thought into how the work may be undertaken given your location and what support you may require.

Why you’ll love working with us: 

People First Bank – our new brand – says exactly what we’re all about: people. We're committed to supporting and growing our team and creating an outstanding work environment.

You’ll have access to financial and lifestyle benefits to support your success and individuality:

Flexibility and hybrid working arrangements.

Enhance your salary with discounts on banking, health insurance, and shopping across Australia.

Advance your career with training, study support, and project opportunities.

Competitive leave benefits that empower employees to take time for themselves and loved ones.

Wellbeing support with our Employee Assistance Program, wellbeing leave, mental health app, and mental health ambassadors’ program.

Make a difference with paid volunteering, Workplace Giving, and diverse community initiatives.

Be recognised for your contributions through our peer-driven recognition program.

 

A bit about People First Bank:  

In March 2023, People’s Choice and Heritage Bank merged to form Heritage and People’s Choice Limited - Australia's leading mutual bank.  We are now on an exciting journey to bring together our products, services, and technology under a single brand - People First Bank.

We have over 700,000 members and 1,900 employees, with dual head offices in Adelaide and Toowoomba, an Australian-based contract centre and branches across five states. 

With a vision ‘To deliver positive change through banking’, People First Bank serves members and contributes to the community, while offering an inclusive, flexible work environment. We’re big enough to make an impact; but agile enough to influence and do interesting work.

Company description:



Insignia Financial Limited is an Australian financial services company which offers a range of products and services including financial advice, superannuation, investment management and trustee services



Job description:



Lead one of Australia's fastest-growing wealth platforms at a key moment of scale, investment and transformation.

This is a rare opportunity for an experienced technology executive to lead a modern, successful platform with clear ambition, strong momentum and meaningful investment behind it.

Our Wrap platform (Expand) has established itself is a scalable, stable and high-performing foundation for growth, built on our proprietary technology and enabled by experienced delivery teams. The platform strength has been reflected in Wrap's rapid rise to 4th in the market and its clear ambition to become the market leader.

This role offers an experienced senior leader the opportunity to build on that foundation, sharpen the platform's commercial edge, scale capability, lift productivity and set a clear strategic view on where to invest next to support continued growth.

The opportunity to join the team
As General Manager Technology for the Wrap business, you will have end-to-end accountability for the technology function supporting one of Australia's largest and fastest growing wealth platforms managing over $100 billion in funds. Reporting to Liz McCarthy (CEO MLC Expand), you will operate as the business units senior technology leader, setting and executing a clear technology strategy aligned to Wrap's growth and commercial objectives.

You will lead the approximately 160 people across onshore and offshore teams. The group has a strong track record of delivery; the next phase is to lift engagement, build momentum, and evolve the operating model to support faster growth and higher productivity. You will also lead effectively across the organisation, partnering with senior leaders and teams across our business to deliver and continuously improve innovative retirement solutions at scale.

A key focus of the role is enabling adviser efficiency and client confidence through technology. You will own the technology roadmap, prioritise investments that make it easier for advisers to serve clients, and use data, AI and automation to simplify processes, improve decision-making and strengthen the client experience.

With the right investment and a large change agenda, this is a rare opportunity to shape how technology directly supports advisers, clients and the future of retirement in Australia.

What You Bring to This Role
This is a senior GM-level role suited to an established technology leader. To be successful, you will bring:

Proven experience leading large, complex technology functions with full accountability for strategy, delivery and operations, underpinned by a strong personal background in technology
Strong technical credibility and a deep understanding of modern platforms and the broader technology landscape
A track record of setting clear direction, prioritising investment, and delivering complex change in regulated environments
Experience building and leading high-performing, hybrid teams across onshore and offshore models
Practical, credible experience leveraging data and AI to lift productivity and capability
Strong stakeholder leadership and influencing skills, with the ability to partner effectively at executive level
A leadership style grounded in listening, coaching and clarity, with the confidence to make decisions and drive performance

Industry experience in regulated environments such as financial services, wealth or superannuation is highly valued, particularly where pace, scale and commercial agility have been critical.

Find the better way

What gets us excited about working here? Helping people feel good about their money as one of Australia's leading financial wellbeing organisations. From those starting out to those retiring. As your team, we'll show up for you and together we will create financial wellbeing for every Australian. Because we know people who are confident about their financial future live better lives.

If this sounds like your kind of career, you sound like our kind of person. With us, you'll do work that builds your technical know-how and challenges our entire industry to move forward. Along the way, you'll be free to explore new ideas and technology, solve problems in a team, and independently to get great things done. Around here, we like to call it "putting our fingerprints on the future".

People-inspired tech

Deep expertise combined with creativity can take on the trickiest of problems. Join our team of brilliant minds and mentors as we explore our way to innovation and apply technology in more human ways than ever.

__

To Apply

Read more here about why you should join our team.

Applicants will be required to provide evidence of their eligibility to work in Australia, and at a minimum be required to undertake police and basic credit checks as a condition of employment.

Please note that applications from agencies will not be considered at this time.

We acknowledge and celebrate the richness that individual differences bring to our team. If you need assistance or an adjustment during the application process, please reach out and let us know.
