Job Summary:

We are looking for an experienced SAP CRM Functional Consultant with strong Utilities domain expertise to design, configure, and deliver SAP CRM solutions aligned with business and industry best practices. The role involves close collaboration with business and IT stakeholders to translate business needs into scalable SAP CRM solutions, with a strong focus on Utilities sales, service, and customer interaction processes.

Key Responsibilities

Lead Fit‑Gap analysis and functional workshops with Business and IT stakeholders.

Translate business requirements into functional and solution designs aligned with SAP CRM Utilities best practices.

Provide functional leadership and advisory support on SAP CRM architecture and solution design.

Bridge functional and technical gaps by collaborating closely with ABAP and integration teams.

Identify, design, and implement SAP CRM solutions that meet business objectives and quality standards.

Produce and maintain high‑quality Functional Design Documents (FDDs) and Solution Design documentation.

Support system configuration, testing, defect resolution, and production issues.

Act as a key point of contact for functional decision‑making and stakeholder communication.

Required Skills & Qualifications

Strong functional expertise in SAP CRM within the Utilities domain.

Experience working with at least one SAP IS‑U module and CRM–IS‑U integrations.

Proven ability to conduct Fit‑Gap analysis and lead stakeholder workshops.

Strong analytical and problem‑solving skills.

Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills, including interaction with senior business and IT leaders.

Ability to work effectively across cross‑functional teams and distributed delivery models.

Collaborative mindset with a strong focus on solution quality and business outcomes.

Benefits:

Joining Cognizant will give you the opportunity to learn and collaborate with some of the most talented people in the industry, while having your finger on the pulse of emerging industry trends and working on the cutting edge of technology in your field of expertise.

We recognize that our people perform at their best when they feel valued as significant contributors and that is why at Cognizant, taking care of our employees is a priority:

·   You can pursue innovative career tracks and opportunities here

·   You can enhance your professional development through education and dedicated training

·   We’ll give you the skills you need to keep pace with the changing workplace while our compensation, benefits and wellness packages help you stay healthy and plan for the future.

Next Steps:

Please reach out to our friendly and welcoming team today to apply and register your interest for this full-time role. We'd love to help you get your next role and enable you to fulfil your professional ambitions.

Job Summary
Join a forward-thinking team as an Senior Investment Analyst, where your expertise will shape governance standards across multiple RSEs. This role offers the opportunity to lead investment policy management, provide strategic oversight, and influence trustee decision-making. Ideal for an experienced professional looking to make a tangible impact within a respected financial services environment.

Benefits

Lead the development and refinement of the Trustee Investment Governance Framework (IGF), ensuring it remains aligned with evolving legislative standards and industry best practices.
Engage directly with senior stakeholders and investment managers to shape investment strategies and approval processes.
Take ownership of due diligence processes, preparing reports that influence trustee decisions across a diverse asset base.
Contribute to ongoing product enhancements and new offerings, supporting the trustee’s commitment to client outcomes.
Be part of a high-calibre team, operating within a structured environment that values governance, compliance, and precise reporting.
Work in a role with significant influence on investment policies, with clear pathways for professional growth and development.
Key Responsibilities

Establish and maintain the Trustee Investment Governance Framework (IGF) for all RSEs.
Monitor adherence to policies, frameworks, and legislative compliance across the investment lifecycle.
Assist in annual reviews of investment strategies, including stress testing under SPS 530.
Provide expert support and strategic advice on investment issues to trustee teams and clients.
Lead the investment approval process, ensuring consistent standards are applied to all decisions.
Liaise with asset consultants and investment managers to ensure compliance and high standards in reporting and oversight.
Requirements

Tertiary qualification in Finance, Commerce, Economics or a related discipline.
Minimum of 5 years’ experience in financial services, with a focus on investment monitoring and governance.
Proven experience establishing and maintaining trustee investment frameworks (IGF).
Strong report-writing skills and the ability to provide clear, actionable advice.
Demonstrable knowledge of APRA’s SPS 530 standards and legislative requirements.
Experience working directly with investment managers and fund consultants.

Identify opportunities to uplift the risk, control and data governance environment, aligned to frameworks and best practice.

Ability to work flexibly within a small, high‑performing team

Job Title: Principal Planning Analyst, Strategy and Planning Team
Company: Cairns & Hinterland Hospital and Health Service
Category:
Strategy & Planning (Consulting & Strategy)
Government - State (Government & Defence)
Ad Text:
About us:

The Strategy and Planning team provide strategic leadership, strategic direction and coordination for planning initiatives across Cairns and Hinterland Hospital and Health Service, to meet the current and future health care needs of our community.

Cairns Hospital is the largest major hospital in Far North Queensland, caring for patients from the Mossman/Port Douglas to Tully in the south and out to Croydon in the west. 

Our population is 260,000 with First Nations people making up 14% of the region's population. Cairns Hospital provides a range of specialist outpatient, inpatient, and emergency services from its CBD site on the Cairns Esplanade. The hospital has 506 inpatient beds and 53 emergency department treatment spaces, 16 ICU beds, 22 special care nursery beds, 48 inpatient mental health beds and 25 maternity beds.

Cairns offers a widely-varied lifestyle in a tropical environment boasting several local landmarks and is the perfect base from which to explore the tropical north. Popular activities include exploring the World Heritage-listed Great Barrier Reef and the Daintree rainforest, as well as the Atherton Tablelands to the west of town and the Port Douglas region to the north. Cairns is also a well-known venue for sporting events, such as mountain biking competitions and marathon festivals.

We're committed to improving the health and wellbeing of people in Far North Queensland by providing high quality acute health care services.

About your role:

Extract large data sets from multiple sources, analyse and interpret results to inform planning requirements for population health projections, local needs assessment, geographical or health infrastructure planning, specific population group planning, health demand and scenario impact projections.
Undertake research and environmental scanning activities to proactively support the delivery of innovative, enhanced/ alternative models of care, or service re-orientation in line with local, state, and national directives.
Manage and coordinate designated data analysis projects, undertaking responsibility for planning, scoping, and scheduling activities, and completing projects in accordance with agreed timeframes and deliverables.
Provide analysis and feedback on Federal and State policy and planning documents, such as guidelines, frameworks, benchmarks and ensure their correct application when producing planning artefacts.

For more information about this role, please refer to the attached Role Description.

Vaccine Preventable Diseases (VPD) evidence as required for your employment in accordance with legislation/government policy and Directives.

What we offer:

Cairns and Hinterland Hospital and Health service offer a number of workplace benefits. Besides attractive remuneration, generous superannuation, salary packaging and career development opportunities, we value a healthy work/life balance that allows you take advantage of the sunshine, hiking tracks, swimming spots, reef and rainforest. 

Learn what it's like to work with us, in an inclusive and supportive work environment.

Discover our dynamic and culturally diverse region through community and lifestyle.

Listen to what some of our staff have to say about working in our regions.

Visit our website to learn more about our hospitals and health centres.
